data.title: Yuk Wing to Acquire 65% Stake in Anhui Power Technology Firm, Adds Call Option for Further Expansion
data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Yuk Wing Group Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1536) ) just unveiled an update.

Yuk Wing Group Holdings Ltd. has agreed, through a wholly owned subsidiary, to acquire a 65% equity interest in Anhui Runsheng Electric Power Technology Co., Ltd. for RMB37.5 million, with the target to become an indirect non-wholly owned subsidiary whose financial results will be consolidated into the group’s accounts. The deal is classified as a major transaction under Hong Kong listing rules and will be put to shareholders at an extraordinary general meeting, alongside approval for a call option that allows Yuk Wing to buy additional equity within 12 months, signaling a potential deepening of its stake and future expansion of the enlarged group.

Under the sale and purchase agreement, the seller will grant Yuk Wing a call option over further shares in the target at a nominal upfront consideration, with a total exercise price of RMB20.1 million if fully used. The company plans to send a circular with financial details of the target and pro forma information on the enlarged group by early July 2026, as it seeks to strengthen its position in the power technology segment and provide greater clarity to investors on the acquisition’s financial impact.

More about Yuk Wing Group Holdings Ltd.

Yuk Wing Group Holdings Ltd. is a Hong Kong-listed company that expands its portfolio through strategic acquisitions and investments. The group operates via wholly owned subsidiaries and focuses on consolidating newly acquired businesses into its existing operations to drive growth and enhance shareholder value.

Average Trading Volume: 594,200

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$141.4M

data.title: China Resources Land Growth in Sales Value and Rental Income for May 2026
data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

China Resources Land ( (HK:1109) ) has shared an update.

China Resources Land reported that in May 2026 it achieved gross contracted sales of about RMB23.51 billion, a 28.1% year-on-year increase, even as contracted gross floor area fell 19.7% to 0.521 million square metres. For the first five months of 2026, sales reached RMB93.51 billion, up 7.7% year-on-year, with contracted GFA down 26.6%, suggesting higher average selling prices or a shift in project mix amid a softer volume environment.

The group’s recurring revenue in May rose 8.6% year-on-year to RMB4.44 billion, driven by an 11.4% rise in rental income to RMB3.00 billion from its investment properties. Cumulative recurring revenue for the first five months climbed 7.7% to RMB22.01 billion, with rental income up 12.9% to RMB15.00 billion, underscoring the growing contribution of stable rental streams to the company’s earnings profile despite volatility in contracted sales volumes.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1109) stock is a Buy
with a HK$34.90 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Resources Land stock,
see the HK:1109 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Resources Land

China Resources Land Limited is a mainland China-focused real estate developer and property operator listed in Hong Kong, engaged in residential development and investment property rental businesses. The group generates revenue from contracted property sales as well as recurring income from a sizable portfolio of investment properties, positioning it as a major player in China’s urban commercial and residential real estate markets.

Average Trading Volume: 23,219,065

TransThera Sciences (Nanjing). Inc. ( (HK:2617) ) has issued an announcement.

TransThera Sciences (Nanjing), Inc. is a biopharmaceutical company based in the People’s Republic of China and listed in Hong Kong, focusing on the development and commercialization of pharmaceutical products. The group operates across multiple subsidiaries and emphasizes commercial execution in its drug portfolio as it competes in China’s fast-growing biotech and pharmaceutical markets.

data.description:

Pharmaron Beijing Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3759) ) has issued an update.

Pharmaron Beijing Co., Ltd. reported the results of its 2025 annual general meeting in Beijing, confirming that the meeting was validly convened under PRC law and its Articles of Association, with about 48.7% of voting shares represented. All directors attended, and independent scrutineers, including the H share registrar and the company’s PRC legal adviser, supervised the poll process in line with Hong Kong listing requirements.

Shareholders approved all ordinary resolutions, including the 2025 profit distribution and 2026 interim dividend plan, the 2025 annual report and results, director remuneration and a new remuneration policy, an estimated hedging quota for 2026, and the appointment of domestic and international auditors. The meeting also endorsed the election of a non-executive director to the fourth board and amendments to the Articles of Association, reinforcing Pharmaron’s governance framework and capital management plans for the coming year.

Hengxin Technology Ltd. ( (HK:1085) ) has shared an update.

Hengxin Technology’s subsidiary Qinghai Zhongkong has signed three major contracts with companies from GCL Group for the Delingha 350 MW solar thermal project in Qinghai, with a total value of about RMB4.73 billion. The agreements cover construction works by GCL Green Energy, supply of key equipment by Lvyuan Xinneng, and installation and commissioning services by GCL Green Energy, marking a significant expansion of Hengxin’s exposure to large-scale solar thermal infrastructure in China.

Because the aggregated transaction size exceeds Hong Kong listing thresholds, the deals are classified as a very substantial acquisition and will require shareholder approval at an extraordinary general meeting. The company plans to issue a circular with further details by early July 2026, and has cautioned shareholders and potential investors that the transactions may not proceed if approval is not obtained, underscoring governance requirements and execution risk around this large-scale investment.

More about Hengxin Technology Ltd.

Hengxin Technology Ltd., incorporated in Singapore and listed in Hong Kong, operates through subsidiaries such as Qinghai Zhongkong and is expanding into large-scale renewable energy infrastructure. The company is pursuing growth in utility-scale solar thermal projects in mainland China, positioning itself within the broader clean energy value chain.

data.title: Zhengye International Details Market-Based Pricing for Industrial Property Disposal
data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Zhengye International Holdings Co., Ltd. ( (HK:3363) ) has provided an update.

Zhengye International Holdings has provided additional details on the pricing of a disclosed disposal of an industrial property in Zhongshan City, clarifying how the consideration was set. The transaction involves the first floor of a factory building with a gross floor area of about 1,208 square metres and a total price of roughly RMB7.88 million, translating to a unit price of about RMB6,523 per square metre.

The company states that the price was reached after arm’s length negotiations and benchmarking against four comparable first-floor industrial units in the same project and nearby areas, while adjusting for an estimated 10% discount from asking prices to actual transaction levels. Management emphasises that the agreed unit price falls within the prevailing market range once factors such as floor level, ceiling height, floor loading capacity and access for logistics operations are taken into account, suggesting the disposal is aligned with market valuations and supports transparent asset pricing for stakeholders.

More about Zhengye International Holdings Co., Ltd.

Zhengye International Holdings Company Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating through a group structure in the industrial property sector in Zhongshan City, China. The group’s portfolio includes factory buildings with logistics, loading and industrial-use features, positioning it within the broader industrial and manufacturing infrastructure market.

data.description:

China Vanke Co ( (HK:2202) ) has issued an announcement.

China Vanke has provided an update on its planned disposal of a 99.41% equity stake in its subsidiary Huanshan via a public tender on the Shenzhen Assets Exchange. The Hong Kong Stock Exchange approved the company’s application on how to calculate the profits ratio, confirming the transaction as a discloseable transaction subject to announcement and reporting obligations under Chapter 14 of the Listing Rules.

The company reported that the public tender process ended on 11 June 2026 without any bidder meeting the required conditions, resulting in the termination of the tender. The board stated that this termination will not materially affect the group’s financial position or operations and said Vanke will continue to explore alternative arrangements for disposing of the Huanshan equity interest in line with market conditions and strategic considerations, while cautioning investors in trading its shares.

BHCC Holding Ltd. said its indirect wholly owned unit BHCC Project 2, together with partner ECO Energy, has submitted a joint tender to lease a land parcel at Kranji Close, Singapore, to develop a civic and community institution in the form of a foreign workers’ dormitory. The tender requires a deposit of about S$3.5 million, funded from internal resources, which will serve as a security deposit if the bid succeeds and will be refunded within 14 days after award in the case of an unsuccessful bid.

The company noted that the payment of the BHCC Deposit represents more than 5% but less than 25% under Hong Kong’s listing percentage ratio tests, classifying it as a discloseable transaction subject to reporting and announcement obligations. data.title: China Rare Earth Hit by Senior Resignations, Falls Out of HK Listing Compliance
data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

An announcement from China Rare Earth Holdings ( (HK:0769) ) is now available.

China Rare Earth Holdings has announced the resignation of acting board chair and executive director Ms. Guo Jinying, executive director Mr. Kang Ding, independent non-executive director Prof. Yip Tze Wai Albert, and company secretary and authorised representative Ms. Tsui Mei Fung, all citing the need to devote more time to other business commitments. The departing directors stated they have no disagreements with the board, and the company expressed appreciation for their contributions.

The departures leave China Rare Earth in breach of multiple Hong Kong listing rules relating to the required number and qualifications of independent non-executive directors, the composition of audit, remuneration and nomination committees, and the appointment of a company secretary and authorised representatives. The company said it will use its best efforts to identify suitable candidates and fill the vacancies as soon as practicable to restore full compliance, with further announcements to be made when appropriate.

More about China Rare Earth Holdings

China Rare Earth Holdings Limited operates in the rare earth and related materials sector, focusing on the production and sale of rare earth products and associated industrial materials. The company is listed in Hong Kong and is subject to the corporate governance and compliance requirements of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$1.95B

An announcement from Chengdu Expressway Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1785) ) is now available.

Chengdu Expressway Co., Ltd. has approved a plan to convert 331,220,400 unlisted domestic shares, representing 20% of its total share capital and currently held by Chengdu Communications Investment Transportation Construction and Management Group Co., Ltd., into H shares listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. The board noted that shareholder approval is not required under the company’s articles, and it cautioned investors that the plan is at an early stage, with detailed implementation yet to be finalised and further announcements to follow, underscoring regulatory and execution risks for stakeholders.

data.title: Chenming Paper Sells Yujing Hotel Stake in RMB316.7 Million Exit
data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

An update from Shandong Chenming Paper Holdings Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1812) ) is now available.

Shandong Chenming Paper Holdings is further streamlining its portfolio as a wholly owned subsidiary, Chenming Paper Sales, agreed to sell a 90.05% stake in Yujing Hotel and related debt claims to Shouguang Cultural Tourism Investment for RMB316.7 million. The deal removes the hotel business from the group’s consolidated accounts, underscoring a continued shift away from non-core hospitality assets toward its core paper operations.

Under the equity and debt transfer agreement signed on 12 June 2026, the buyer will assume both the majority equity interest and RMB199.5 million of debt exposures, with payment due in a single cash instalment within 90 days. Classified as a discloseable transaction under Hong Kong listing rules, the disposal highlights Chenming’s effort to deleverage and reallocate capital, potentially improving balance-sheet transparency and focusing resources on its main manufacturing business.

More about Shandong Chenming Paper Holdings Co., Ltd. Class H

Shandong Chenming Paper Holdings Limited is a mainland Chinese paper manufacturer, primarily engaged in producing and selling pulp and various paper products. The company serves domestic and international markets and also holds ancillary assets, including non-core holdings such as hospitality-related investments, which it has been gradually rationalising to sharpen its industrial focus.

data.description:

  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Shenzhen Investment Holdings Bay Area Development Company ( (HK:0737) ) has provided an announcement.

Shenzhen Investment Holdings Bay Area Development Company Limited has agreed to issue 250,000,000 new shares, representing about 8.11% of its existing share capital, to an independent subscriber under its general mandate. The shares will be priced at HK$1.8335 each, reflecting a roughly 5% discount to the recent market price, and will not require separate shareholder approval.

The placement is expected to raise gross proceeds of HK$458.375 million, with net proceeds of about HK$456 million after expenses. Management plans to allocate around 60% of the funds to the reconstruction and expansion of the Guangzhou-Shenzhen section of the Beijing-Hong Kong-Macao Expressway and around 40% to potential land development along that route, other business expansion and general working capital, signalling a push to strengthen core infrastructure assets and related development opportunities.

More about Shenzhen Investment Holdings Bay Area Development Company

Shenzhen Investment Holdings Bay Area Development Company Limited is a Cayman Islands–incorporated infrastructure developer focused on expressway assets in the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area. The company’s core operations include investment, reconstruction, expansion and land development along key expressway corridors such as the Guangzhou-Shenzhen section of the Beijing-Hong Kong-Macao Expressway.

data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.

China Resources Pharmaceutical Group Ltd. ( (HK:3320) ) just unveiled an update.

China Resources Pharmaceutical Group’s shareholders approved all resolutions at the 12 June 2026 annual general meeting, including adoption of the 2025 financial statements and a final dividend of RMB0.122 per share. Investors also backed the re-election of executive, non-executive and independent directors, signalling continued confidence in the existing leadership team.

The meeting authorised the board to set director and auditor remuneration, reappointed KPMG as auditor and granted mandates to repurchase and issue new shares, with an extension of the issuance mandate. These approvals give the company flexibility in capital management and fundraising, supporting potential future buy-backs and equity issuance to strengthen its balance sheet and fund growth initiatives.

More about China Resources Pharmaceutical Group Ltd.

China Resources Pharmaceutical Group Ltd. is a Hong Kong-incorporated pharmaceutical company engaged in the manufacture, distribution and sale of medicines and healthcare products. The group focuses on China’s pharmaceutical and healthcare markets, operating across the value chain from production to retail to serve hospitals, pharmacies and consumers.
